item.description: Hundreds of protesters clashed with riot police in Venice as billionaire American ambassador Tilman Fertitta arrived aboard his luxury yacht, the Boardwalk, during a diplomatic tour marking the 250th anniversary of American independence. Demonstrators criticized the display of U.S. wealth and influence, arguing it undermines Italy's post-WWII international order under the Trump administration. Protesters carried signs like 'Venezia non si USA' (Venice is not to be used) and 'Make America Read Again,' while clashing with police near the yacht. Fertitta, owner of the Houston Rockets and a hospitality mogul, defended the tour as celebrating U.S.-Italy ties but faced backlash over his $11.3 billion net worth and perceived arrogance. Activists linked the event to broader criticism of Trump's foreign policies, including strikes on Iran that they claim spiked energy prices. The yacht, which dwarfed Venice's skyline, drew outrage for its size and symbolism of global power dynamics.

item.title: China's open-source AI model sparks market volatility
item.description: The release of an open-source artificial intelligence model developed by a Chinese company has led to significant market volatility, with investors reacting to potential implications for global tech competition. The model, which is designed to rival Western AI advancements, has prompted concerns about intellectual property risks and geopolitical tensions. Financial markets saw a sharp decline in tech stocks, particularly those associated with AI research and development. Analysts suggest the selloff reflects broader anxieties about China's growing influence in the tech sector and its ability to challenge established players. The incident highlights the increasing interconnectedness of global technology ecosystems and the sensitivity of markets to shifts in innovation leadership. While the model's capabilities remain under evaluation, its open-source nature has raised questions about security vulnerabilities and the potential for misuse. Regulators in multiple countries are reportedly m

item.title: Judge Orders Hearing on States' Request to Block Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger
item.description: A federal judge heard arguments from a coalition of states seeking to block the Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger, citing antitrust concerns over market concentration in theatrical and cable industries. The states argue the merged entity would dominate up to 30% of top-grossing films and control 50 of 189 basic cable channels, stifling competition. Paramount's attorney, Jeffrey Kessler, countered that new entrants like Apple and Amazon are already disrupting the industry, citing recent hits such as 'F1' and 'Project Hail Mary.' The judge expressed openness to a temporary restraining order, emphasizing the difficulty of reversing the merger if ruled unlawful. Key legal battles center on market definitions, with Paramount challenging the states' metrics for anticompetitive behavior. A $7 million daily penalty looms if the deal proceeds past September 30, adding urgency to the case. The hearing highlighted tensions between legacy studios and emerging tech firms in shaping media mark

item.title: Davido Supports Israel DMW During Father's Burial, Fans Praise His Loyalty
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Davido made headlines by arriving in Nigeria on a private jet to attend the burial of his aide Israel DMW's father, sparking widespread admiration online. The heartwarming airport reunion, captured in viral footage, showed Davido and his crew being welcomed by an emotional Israel DMW, who expressed joy at seeing his boss. Fans praised Davido as 'the best boss ever,' highlighting his dedication to his team during a difficult time. The incident underscores the strong bond between Davido and Israel DMW, who has been a trusted aide for years. Social media users flooded comment sections with messages of support, emphasizing the rarity of such employer-employee loyalty. The article also notes Davido's previous interactions with Israel DMW, including sharing videos of his visa process, further cementing their relationship. This event reflects broader themes of camaraderie and professional integrity in Nigerian entertainment circles.

item.title: SpaceX and Pentagon Discuss Potential AI Collaboration
item.description: The article discusses ongoing negotiations between SpaceX, the aerospace manufacturer led by Elon Musk, and the U.S. Department of Defense regarding a potential collaboration on artificial intelligence initiatives. While specific details of the proposed agreement remain undisclosed, sources indicate that the partnership could involve joint research and development efforts to advance AI technologies for military applications. The Pentagon's interest in AI is driven by its desire to enhance operational efficiency, improve decision-making processes, and maintain technological superiority over potential adversaries. SpaceX, known for its expertise in rocketry and satellite technology, may contribute its engineering capabilities to develop AI-driven systems for defense purposes. However, the deal faces scrutiny from privacy advocates and ethical experts who warn about the risks of weaponizing AI. The collaboration could also have broader implications for the commercial space industry, poten

item.title: Abdul El-Sayed Discusses AIPAC Spending and Defunding Police Policies
item.description: The article features Abdul El-Sayed, a political commentator, discussing his views on AIPAC's spending priorities, the 'defund the police' movement, and clarifying his political stance as not being a socialist. He addresses the intersection of foreign policy funding and domestic policing reforms, emphasizing nuanced approaches to systemic issues. El-Sayed critiques the allocation of resources to organizations like AIPAC while advocating for reallocating funds to community-based solutions. The conversation highlights tensions within progressive politics, balancing calls for police reform with broader economic and social policies. He also refutes labels of being a socialist, positioning himself as a pragmatic policymaker focused on incremental change rather than radical overhauls. The discussion underscores the complexities of navigating political discourse in an era of polarized debates.

item.title: ICE contractor shoots protester during workplace dispute, faces murder charges
item.description: An employee at an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention facility in Aurora, Colorado, shot a protester outside the facility on Thursday night, according to local reports. The incident occurred when Brandon Booth, a 42-year-old employee of The GEO Group (a private prison company contracted by ICE), confronted two women who were photographing his vehicle. Booth retrieved his personally owned pistol and fired a single shot, striking one of the women in the lower body. The protester, who is expected to survive, was found by responding officers along with an unharmed companion. Booth was not present at the scene but was later detained in his vehicle after fleeing the area. Aurora Police Department stated that Booth and other employees were waiting outside due to protests impeding their access to the facility. The GEO Group confirmed that one of its off-duty employees was involved in the incident. Police Chief Todd Chamberlain described the event as a "tragedy on all fronts." T

item.title: BTS Member Teases Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ahead of Unpacked Event
item.description: A member of the popular boy band group BTS, J-Hope, recently shared a video teasing one of Samsung’s upcoming foldables, which appears to be the Galaxy Z Fold 8. This comes ahead of Samsung’s highly anticipated Galaxy Unpacked event scheduled for July 22 in London, where the Fold 8 series and Flip 8 will debut. The teaser, posted during a stop on BTS's global tour, features J-Hope holding the device with a peace sign, offering a real-world look at the foldable. While no detailed hands-on review is provided, leaks suggest the Galaxy Z Fold 8 will feature a 7.6-inch QHD+ display with a 4:3 aspect ratio, a 5.5-inch cover screen with a 16:10 ratio, a 4,800mAh battery, 12GB of RAM, and storage options up to 1TB. The device is expected to start at $1,899 upon release. This collaboration between Samsung and BTS highlights the growing influence of K-pop artists in global marketing strategies, blending entertainment with technology promotion.

item.title: Israeli military actions in Gaza raise concerns over civilian casualties and international law violations
item.description: A recent article from July 17, 2026, discusses escalating tensions between Israel and Hamas in the Gaza Strip, with allegations of widespread civilian casualties and potential war crimes. The piece highlights reports of extensive damage to infrastructure, displacement of civilians, and humanitarian crises exacerbated by ongoing conflict. While Israeli authorities maintain that their actions are proportionate and aimed at neutralizing militant threats, international organizations and human rights groups have condemned the scale of destruction as disproportionate and potentially constituting genocide under international law. The article references historical precedents of civilian harm in conflicts involving Israel and calls for greater accountability and adherence to humanitarian principles. It also notes the complex geopolitical context, including regional power dynamics and global diplomatic efforts to broker a ceasefire. Despite these concerns, the Israeli government has dismissed ac

item.title: Trump Reacts to Wildfire Smoke Impacting U.S. Air Quality
item.description: President Donald Trump escalated tensions with Canada on July 17, 2026, after wildfires and smoke from the northern neighbor caused significant air quality issues in the U.S. Midwest and Northeast. In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump accused Canada of failing to manage its forests and brush, leading to the 'unacceptable' invasion of polluted air into American skies. He threatened to hold Canada responsible, claiming the country's negligence costs the U.S. billions annually and demanded that tariffs be adjusted to cover these expenses. Trump also announced plans to call Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney to address the issue. The article highlights the broader context of cross-border environmental concerns, noting that Canada had previously offered assistance to California during wildfires in 2025. Critics argue that Trump's outburst reflects a pattern of politicizing environmental issues, while others emphasize the real health risks posed by the smoke. The incident underscor

item.title: Technical Insights from Rebuilding a macOS Gaming Diagnostics App in Swift
item.description: The article details the author's experience in improving a macOS gaming diagnostics tool called CloudBoost. Initially, the app struggled to provide actionable insights into cloud gaming instability issues like sudden latency spikes or network fluctuations. The revised version focuses on explaining session-specific problems rather than acting as a generic 'game booster.' Key updates include real-time monitoring of signals such as latency, network path changes, Wi-Fi activity, and thermal pressure, which are transformed into a timeline with severity ratings, potential causes, and suggested troubleshooting steps. For example, instead of vague alerts like 'network unstable,' the app now recommends testing Ethernet connections or disabling VPNs temporarily. The tool also applies reversible temporary adjustments during sessions, such as refreshing DNS states or adjusting background traffic, without terminating user applications. CloudBoost PRO, a paid version, adds automation, advanced diagn

item.title: U.S.-Iran Negotiations Stall as Military Escalation Continues
item.description: The U.S. and Iran's 60-day negotiation period to resolve tensions over nuclear programs, sanctions, and freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz has reached its halfway point. Despite initial hopes for diplomacy, the agreement has collapsed into escalating military conflict. The Trump administration has intensified strikes on Iranian military targets, including coastal defenses, missile storage sites, and naval capabilities, following repeated Iranian attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran has retaliated with missile and drone strikes against U.S. allies in the Gulf, including attacks on Kuwait's power and desalination plants. Trump has framed the conflict as a test of Iran's willingness to negotiate, warning of further strikes if talks fail. The White House has abandoned key concessions, such as a temporary oil sales waiver for Iran, and reinstated economic sanctions. Analysts suggest the U.S. views military pressure as a prerequisite for diplomacy, though ex

item.title: Leon Black Testifies About Epstein's Role in Nondisclosure Agreements
item.description: Newly released transcripts from a House Oversight Committee interview reveal billionaire Leon Black's panicked attempts to avoid discussing nondisclosure agreements (NDAs) linked to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Black, chairman of Apollo Global Management, invoked attorney-client privilege repeatedly, forcing committee members to issue subpoenas. The interview, conducted in May 2026, shows Black denying any advisory role in creating NDAs with Epstein, who allegedly received over $170 million for 'tax and estate planning advice' from the 1990s onward. Black admitted discussing at least one NDA with Epstein but claimed the deceased sex offender had no involvement in its creation. He also revealed paying a woman over $21 million over 15 years after she allegedly 'blackmailed and extorted' him, stating Epstein helped manage his finances. Black denied being a close friend of Epstein's, calling the term 'amorphous,' and insisted their relationship was strictly professional. His att

item.title: Dyson Hot+Cool HF1: A Year-Round Smart Appliance Review
item.description: The Dyson Hot+Cool HF1 is a 2-in-1 smart appliance that provides heating in winter and cooling in summer, offering year-round comfort. Priced at $499, it combines Dyson's bladeless design with smart controls, allowing users to operate it via smartphone, remote, or onboard buttons. Key features include quiet operation (26 decibels in Sleep mode), adjustable airflow direction, and an intelligent thermostat that maintains desired temperatures. While the cooling mode functions as a high-powered fan rather than an air conditioner, its smart scheduling and automation capabilities make it convenient for daily use. The bladeless design simplifies cleaning and enhances safety for households with children or pets. Despite its premium price, the HF1 is positioned as a worthwhile investment for replacing traditional space heaters and ceiling fans, offering energy efficiency and multifunctionality in a compact, lightweight unit.

item.title: Tragic Accident: 2-Year-Old Dies After Finding Firearm at Home
item.description: A 2-year-old boy tragically shot and killed himself in a firearm accident at his home in Highland, San Bernardino County, according to authorities. The incident occurred on Thursday night when the child, who was with his mother, Jamaria Cobb, and her boyfriend, Armand Bright, discovered a gun between two seat cushions on a couch. Deputies arrived at the scene around 5:27 p.m. and performed CPR on the child, but he succumbed to his injuries after being transported to a hospital. Investigators confirmed the child's actions led to the accident, and both Cobb and Bright were arrested for child abuse causing great bodily injury or death. The couple is being held at the San Bernardino County Central Detention Center with a $1.35 million bail. A second child in the home was placed under the care of child and family services. Authorities are urging anyone with information to contact the Specialized Investigations Division at (909) 890-4904 or anonymously via We-Tip at (844) 909-3006. This inci

item.title: Trump's Plan to Replace Illegal Truck Drivers with Veterans Could Impact Indian Immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump has announced plans to replace illegal immigrant truck drivers with American war veterans, a policy that could significantly impact Indian immigrants in the U.S. The decision follows a fatal accident in Pennsylvania where a commercial vehicle struck and killed Trooper Michael E. Pahira Jr., with the driver, Michael Bon, charged with multiple offenses including homicide by vehicle. Trump criticized illegal alien truck drivers for lacking proper qualifications and endangering public safety, proposing automatic eligibility for commercial licenses for veterans. This move is expected to affect approximately 130,000-150,000 Indian truckers from Punjab and Haryana, as per data from the North American Punjabi Truckers Association. The policy aligns with Trump's 'Dalilah Law,' which halted commercial driver licenses for illegal aliens after a fatal crash involving an Indian immigrant named Partap Singh. Recent arrests of Indian truck drivers under 'Operation Checkmate' hi

item.title: Protecting Pets from Wildfire Smoke: Essential Tips for Pet Owners
item.description: As wildfire smoke from Canada spreads across the northern United States, pet owners need to take proactive measures to safeguard their animals' health. The article outlines five critical strategies to mitigate the risks posed by poor air quality. First, limit pets' outdoor exposure until air quality improves, and if outdoor access is necessary, keep it brief to minimize inhalation of harmful particles. Second, use air filters to remove fine particulate matter from indoor environments, but avoid pet masks as they may restrict breathing if the animal panics. Ensure living spaces are well-ventilated by using fans to create continuous air movement, especially in confined areas. Hydration is crucial, as smoky air can dry out pets' respiratory tracts, increasing infection risks. Monitor for signs of distress, such as runny noses, sneezing, coughing, or swollen eyes, and note behavioral changes like fatigue or decreased appetite. These steps emphasize preparedness and vigilance to protect pet

item.title: FCC Commissioner Defends Broadcasters After Trump Demands License Revocation
item.description: President Donald Trump faced backlash from FCC Commissioner Anna Gomez after he demanded the Federal Communications Commission revoke the broadcast licenses of ABC and NBC for refusing to air his primetime address. Gomez, the sole Democratic commissioner on the FCC, criticized Trump's request as a 'naked attempt to bully broadcasters' and emphasized that the FCC has no authority to punish stations for editorial decisions. She argued that the networks' refusal to carry the speech was a legitimate exercise of First Amendment rights, noting that the content remained accessible online. Trump used his address to promote his SAVE America Act, discuss claims about Chinese election interference, and accuse ABC and NBC of political bias. Media analysts and journalists weighed in on Gomez's comments, with some highlighting historical precedents where networks have declined to air speeches from former presidents like Barack Obama and Joe Biden. The incident underscores ongoing tensions between me

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Expected to Linger and Spread This Weekend, Affecting Major Cities
item.description: Wildfire smoke from Canada continued to impact the United States on July 17, 2026, with Detroit, Chicago, and Washington, D.C. ranking among the world's most polluted major cities. Air quality measurements showed dangerously high pollution levels, with D.C. and Baltimore experiencing index values of 281 and 247, respectively, by 6 a.m. Eastern time. The smoky conditions, which began drifting into the region overnight, created very unhealthy air quality, prompting health advisories. The article highlights the widespread effects of the smoke, emphasizing its potential to linger and spread further during the weekend. It also notes the significance of monitoring local forecasts, as the situation could worsen. The piece underscores the environmental and public health risks posed by the wildfire smoke, particularly for urban areas. While the smoke primarily affects the Midwest and eastern U.S., the article suggests that the situation could evolve rapidly, requiring ongoing updates. The sourc

item.title: Republican Lawmaker Criticized for Misrepresenting Canada's Wildfire Response
item.description: Rep. Lisa McClain (R-MI), a House Republican Conference Chair, sparked backlash after criticizing Canada's wildfire response on social media. In a post on X, she claimed Michiganders were suffering from 'Canada's negligence' due to smoke from over 890 fires burning in Canada. However, this contradicts Canada's public support for California during its 2025 wildfires, where the Canadian government offered aid and resources. Social media users swiftly pointed out the hypocrisy, highlighting Canada's previous assistance to the U.S. during natural disasters. For instance, when Michigan and Ontario faced tornadoes and storms from the U.S. Midwest, Canada sent hydro crews to restore power. Critics argue McClain's comments ignore this history, framing the issue as a partisan attack rather than a factual discussion. The incident underscores tensions between U.S. and Canadian policies on environmental crises and highlights how political figures often use such issues for ideological messaging. Mc

item.title: White House Teleprompter Operator Faces Insider Trading Allegations
item.description: Gabriel Perez, the White House teleprompter operator, is under investigation for allegedly using his access to presidential speeches to bet on Kalshi markets. ABC News reports that Perez reportedly made over $100,000 by wagering on specific phrases or topics in speeches, including major events like the State of the Union and the World Economic Forum. Kalshi, a prediction market platform, alerted the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) to suspicious trading activity on its 'Mentions' market, where users bet on words or phrases uttered during public addresses. The CFTC discovered Perez placed bets on over a dozen speeches over three months, including a December primetime address, a January Davos speech, and a March Medal of Honor ceremony. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed Perez has been placed on unpaid administrative leave, with President Trump personally sanctioning the decision. Leavitt stated she was unaware of other staffers engaging in similar activiti

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Mullin threatens legal action against states resisting Trump's election security measures
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin has openly threatened to jail state officials who refuse to cooperate with the Trump administration's midterm election security program. During a press conference, Mullin stated that states not participating in the program would be prioritized for scrutiny, with officials facing fines, penalties, or even prison time. This follows President Trump's recent national address on election security and the administration's efforts to exert control over midterm elections, including threats to withhold federal disaster funds from states that do not comply. Critics have condemned Mullin's remarks as naked intimidation, with political analysts warning of the implications for election integrity and democratic processes. The comments have sparked concerns about the administration's tactics to undermine state autonomy and erode public trust in the electoral system. Journalists and experts have highlighted the potential for abuse in determining what consti

item.title: The Disparity Between Animation's Financial Success and Industry Respect in Hollywood
item.description: The article explores the paradox of Hollywood's reliance on animation for box office success while failing to recognize its artistic value. Despite hits like 'The Super Mario Galaxy Movie' and 'Toy Story 5' breaking global box office records, animators face layoffs and undervaluation. Industry leaders like Travis Knight of Laika and Pixar's Pete Docter argue that animation is often dismissed as 'babysitter material,' despite its critical and financial contributions. The piece highlights the tension between animation's profitability and its lack of respect, with debates over whether animated films should be considered for major awards like Best Picture. While some studios are rethinking their approach, the industry's entrenched biases persist, leaving animators in a precarious position. The article also mentions the potential of new distribution models, like 'Wildwood,' to reshape how animated films are perceived and valued.

item.title: United Airlines Investigates Employee After Video Shows Agent Threatening Passenger With ICE
item.description: United Airlines is investigating an incident at San Francisco International Airport after a video circulated online showing a customer service agent telling a passenger that she might call U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) on him. The footage, which lasts approximately 83 seconds and was shared by the aviation blog View from the Wing, captures an argument between the employee and the customer as both record the exchange on their phones. During the confrontation, the agent says, “Maybe we should call ICE on you,” and follows it by stating that the customer “doesn't act like a citizen.” The passenger repeatedly challenges the remark, asking her to repeat it and daring her to contact ICE. Another United employee attempts to de-escalate the situation by asking whether the police should become involved, but the customer insists that he wants the agent to follow through on her statement about ICE. The disagreement continues, with the employee instructing the customer to leave th

item.title: Rob Lowe Partners With Debt Relief Company to Address Financial Stigma
item.description: Actor Rob Lowe has entered a marketing partnership with Accredited Debt Relief, a debt settlement company that negotiates with creditors to reduce amounts owed for credit card, medical bills, and personal loans. The collaboration aims to challenge the stigma surrounding financial hardship by launching a campaign in August. In one TV spot, Lowe highlights how a family might struggle to afford their son's baseball team fees, emphasizing the emotional weight of financial stress. He stated, "Americans are carrying more debt than ever... We need to talk openly about real solutions." Lowe expressed gratitude for being invited to share stories that reduce stigma and encourage seeking support during financial crises. This partnership follows his previous roles in advertising campaigns, including a humorous DirecTV campaign with rival Comcast complaints. The initiative underscores the growing importance of addressing economic challenges through public dialogue and accessible resources.

item.title: Jackson Suber Surpasses Expectations with Strong Start at British Open
item.description: Jackson Suber, a 26-year-old golfer with no prior major championship experience, stunned the field by capturing the solo lead after a remarkable 5-under 65 in the first round of the British Open at Royal Birkdale. His performance, which included an eagle at the 17th hole, positioned him at -5, one shot ahead of Sungjae Im and Dan Brown. Suber's breakthrough comes after a string of strong finishes in recent tournaments, including T4 at the Canadian Open and fourth at the Byron Nelson. Meanwhile, Bryson DeChambeau rebounded from criticism about his links golf skills by posting a solid 67, while defending champion Scottie Scheffler finished at -2. Rory McIlroy struggled with a 2-over 72, falling behind the cut line. The tournament's leaderboard showcases a mix of seasoned pros and rising talents, with Suber's unexpected dominance making headlines. His victory in the first round marks a significant milestone for a player who had never competed in a major championship before this week.

item.title: A 19th-Century Portrait of Marek Ptasiński, a Polish Peasant Under Serfdom
item.description: This article highlights a rare photograph from 1856 depicting Marek Ptasiński, a 41-year-old Polish peasant from the village of Ptaki near Starogród. Taken by Marcin Olszyński, a young photographer from Warsaw, the image captures rural life during serfdom, a system that legally bound peasants to noble-owned land until its abolition in 1864. The salted paper print, created from a glass collodion negative, is part of the Museum of Warsaw's collection and serves as an important historical document of 19th-century European social conditions. Olszyński’s approach aimed to portray rural society authentically rather than stage idealized scenes. The photograph provides insight into the lived experiences of peasants during a period of significant societal change, reflecting both their daily realities and the broader context of agricultural labor in Poland at the time.

item.title: Scientific Explanation for Wildfire Smoke's Long-Distance Drift
item.description: Wildfire smoke from Canada is increasingly affecting the United States, creating a phenomenon where thick, toxic plumes blot out the sky, turning the air milky-white or orange. This pattern has become a recurring summer feature, driven by out-of-control wildfires in the West. The article explores the scientific mechanisms behind smoke drift, emphasizing atmospheric conditions and wind patterns that allow particles to travel hundreds of miles. Researchers highlight how smoke composition, including fine particulate matter and pollutants, interacts with weather systems to create widespread air quality issues. While the visual impact of smoke is striking, the focus is on understanding the environmental and health implications, such as respiratory risks and climate feedback loops. The article also notes the growing frequency of such events, linking them to climate change and prolonged droughts. Experts warn that without significant mitigation efforts, the scale and intensity of these fires—

item.title: Candid Coffees Unveils Menu Highlights at SM North EDSA Location
item.description: The article discusses the recent opening of Candid Coffees' new branch in SM North EDSA, highlighting its menu offerings and customer experience. The location, part of a growing chain known for its quality coffee and pastries, features a modern interior and a curated selection of beverages. Key points include the introduction of seasonal specials like the 'Mango Tango Latte,' emphasis on sustainable practices through partnerships with local farmers, and the branch's commitment to community engagement through events and promotions. The piece also mentions the strategic location near major shopping centers, making it accessible to a broad customer base. While the original title leans into clickbait tactics by suggesting an exhaustive list of options, the content focuses on the new branch's features and offerings rather than promotional hype. The summary underscores the establishment's efforts to blend quality products with community involvement.

item.title: General Compute secures $400M loan using inference chips as collateral
item.description: General Compute, an AI inference cloud startup, has secured a $400 million loan from Upper90, marking the first deal to use inference-specific chips as collateral. This move reflects growing market interest in cost-effective AI infrastructure, as companies seek alternatives to expensive GPU-based systems. Founded by Finn Puklowski, General Compute leverages SambaNova's SN50 chips, which offer 16x faster inference than traditional GPU clouds while being more energy-efficient. Upper90's CEO Billy Libby highlights the shift from GPU financing to inference chips, citing reduced risks and increased market maturity. The deal underscores broader industry trends toward open-source models and chip alternatives to Nvidia, with companies like Groq and Cerebras gaining traction. This financing signals a fragmentation of Nvidia's dominance in AI infrastructure, offering new options for cost-efficient computation.

item.title: San Francisco Orders Tech Giants to Remove AI Nudification Apps
item.description: San Francisco has issued cease-and-desist letters to Apple and Google, demanding the removal of AI-powered apps that generate nonconsensual nude images. The city’s attorney alleges these applications violate privacy laws and pose significant risks to users. The legal action targets 13 specific apps, which allegedly use deepfake technology to create explicit content without consent. Both companies have responded by stating they are reviewing the claims and will comply with legal requirements. The controversy highlights growing concerns about AI-generated pornography and its societal impact. Lawmakers and privacy advocates argue that such technologies enable exploitation and harm victims, while tech firms face pressure to balance innovation with ethical responsibilities. This case underscores the need for stricter regulations on AI tools that could be weaponized for voyeurism or harassment. The city’s move also reflects broader global efforts to combat deepfake pornography, as government

item.title: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller: Pioneering Nun and First Woman to Earn Computer Science Doctorate
item.description: Sister Mary Kenneth Keller (born Evelyn Marie Keller, December 17, 1913 – January 10, 1985) was a pioneering American Catholic nun, educator, and computer scientist. She became the first woman in the United States to earn a Ph.D. in computer science in 1965 from the University of Wisconsin–Madison. Her work on algorithms for analytic differentiation and contributions to the development of BASIC (Beginner’s All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code) revolutionized early computing accessibility. Keller's journey began with a Bachelor of Science in Mathematics from DePaul University in 1943, followed by advanced studies at Purdue, Michigan, and Dartmouth, where she collaborated with John Kemeny and Thomas Kurtz. After earning her doctorate, she founded Clarke College’s computer science department in 1965, advocating for educational equity through technology. Her legacy includes mentoring students, authoring textbooks on BASIC, and breaking barriers for women in STEM during an era of significa

item.title: Strategies for Securing Pre-Seed Funding Without a Product: Insights from Disrupt 2026 Panel
item.description: The article discusses the challenges faced by pre-seed founders in securing funding amid the surge of AI startups dominating seed investments. At TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, a panel titled 'Winning Pre-Seed Without a Product' addresses how entrepreneurs can pitch compelling ideas without tangible products, leveraging storytelling and conviction to attract investors. The session features speakers like Sandhya Venkatachalam (Axiom Partners), Puneet Agarwal (True Ventures), and Austin Clements (Slauson & Co.), who share insights on navigating the evolving startup landscape. With AI accelerating product development, founders must adapt by emphasizing vision and narrative over immediate deliverables. The event highlights broader themes of operational decision-making, fundraising strategies, and community building within the tech ecosystem. Attendees are encouraged to secure tickets for Disrupt 2026 in San Francisco, where additional workshops and networking opportunities will further explore t

item.title: Federal Rule Limits Foreign Student Visas to Four Years, Impacting California Campuses
item.description: A new federal rule limits most international student stays in the U.S. to four years, ending the previous policy that allowed students to remain for the duration of their degree. This change, effective this fall, requires students to apply for visa extensions with rigorous checks, including biometric screening and background verification. The policy also halves the post-graduation grace period to 30 days and restricts Optional Practical Training (OPT) work authorization, which previously allowed science, tech, engineering, and math graduates to work for up to three years. California, home to the largest international student population in the U.S., faces significant disruption, with universities like UC, USC, and Cal State expressing concerns over the impact on research, education, and global ties. The rule, part of broader Trump-era immigration restrictions, aims to address perceived abuses of the visa system but has raised alarms about potential declines in international enrollment a

item.title: id Software Creative Director Addresses Xbox Layoffs, Affirms Studio's Continued Activity and Upcoming Projects
item.description: id Software's creative director, Hugo Martin, has publicly refuted claims that the studio was 'nerfed into the ground' by Xbox layoffs, asserting that the team remains active and capable of producing high-quality games. Following reports of significant staff cuts at Microsoft's Xbox division, including 96 layoffs at id Software's Texas headquarters and 40 remote roles eliminated, Martin emphasized that the studio is operating at the same staffing level as when it developed the 2016 Doom reboot. He clarified that id Tech, the game engine used by id Software and MachineGames, is 'very much alive and well,' with engineers based in Frankfurt and at MachineGames collaborating extensively. Martin also highlighted the commercial success of Doom: The Dark Ages, a prequel to the 2016 Doom and 2020 Doom Eternal games, stating it meets forecasted expectations. Meanwhile, laid-off employees have protested outside Bethesda offices, with union representatives negotiating exit agreements with Microso

item.title: Trump's Accusations Against China Risk Straining U.S.-China Relations
item.description: President Donald Trump's recent allegations that China illicitly obtained 220 million American voter files have sparked significant diplomatic tension, according to NBC News. The claims, which Trump labeled 'the largest compromise of election data in history,' lack credible evidence and contradict findings from his own CIA Director John Ratcliffe, who issued a 2021 memo dismissing such accusations. China's foreign ministry swiftly rejected the allegations as 'fabricated,' with spokesperson Lin Jian calling them a 'malicious smear' aimed at undermining bilateral relations. The timing of the accusations is particularly sensitive, as the U.S. and China are striving to maintain a fragile trade ceasefire amid deepening geopolitical tensions. Experts warn that Trump's public statements could exacerbate existing frictions, despite his avoidance of direct threats toward Beijing or President Xi Jinping. The controversy highlights the precarious state of U.S.-China relations, where mistrust and

item.title: Jim Acosta Criticizes Trump's 'Deranged Dictator' Speech for Its Chilling Implications
item.description: In a scathing analysis, former CNN anchor Jim Acosta condemned President Donald Trump's recent primetime address as a 'deranged dictator speech' that raised alarming concerns about threats to journalists and media freedom. The speech, which focused on alleged election security flaws, included Trump's accusation that U.S. media outlets were part of a 'plot' to perpetuate voter fraud. Acosta highlighted the danger of Trump's rhetoric, noting his calls to revoke broadcast licenses from ABC and NBC for refusing to air the speech. He emphasized that Trump's claims lack evidence, pointing out that no proof of widespread vote switching from Donald Trump to Joe Biden exists. Acosta also criticized the administration's release of 'unsubstantiated claims' about election fraud, arguing that the speech was filled with conspiracy theories without credible backing. The article underscores the growing concerns about Trump's influence on media and his potential to undermine democratic institutions thr

item.title: South L.A. Gang Member Sentenced for $2.8M Bank Fraud Scheme Using Social Media
item.description: Chase Matthew Griffin, a 26-year-old member of the South L.A. Crips street gang, was sentenced to nine years in federal prison for his role in a $2.8 million bank fraud scheme. Prosecutors revealed that Griffin used Instagram to recruit accomplices, advertising for individuals with bank accounts to grant him access. The scheme involved stealing checks from the mail, altering them, or creating counterfeit versions to appear payable to recruited accomplices. Griffin and co-conspirators would deposit fraudulent checks into accomplices' accounts and race to withdraw funds before banks detected the fraud. In one case, checks worth $84,490 sent to a North Hollywood business were deposited into a JPMorgan Chase account and later converted into counterfeit checks with altered payees. The stolen funds were quickly withdrawn via ATMs, digital transfers, and purchases at a San Bernardino County casino. Griffin pleaded guilty in March 2026 to conspiracy to commit bank fraud and was ordered to pay

item.title: City Closes Mobile Home Parks Amid Financial Struggles, Residents Face Relocation Challenges
item.description: The city of Bell, California, has announced plans to close several mobile home parks due to financial constraints, leaving long-term residents in a precarious situation. Officials cited the need to ensure affordability for low-income tenants, but the decision follows years of mismanagement and legal issues, including a former administrator's prison sentence for embezzlement. Residents like Shirley Sanchez, who has lived in one of the parks for 33 years, fear displacement as rent hikes have already forced many to seek more expensive housing. The city claims taxpayers subsidize low land rents through $1.5 million annual maintenance costs, but residents argue that closure leaves them without viable alternatives. Legal requirements mandate relocation assistance, yet options are scarce due to outdated mobile homes and high moving fees. Yudith Meza, whose parents live in one of the parks, highlights the emotional toll as families face uncertainty about their future. The closures underscore b

item.title: Insurgent Campaign Collapse: Vetting Failures Expose Risks in Recruiting Political Outsiders
item.description: The article details the downfall of Maine gubernatorial candidate Paul Platner, whose campaign collapsed due to inadequate vetting by his consultants. Despite initial efforts by a research firm to scrutinize Platner's digital footprint—including problematic Reddit posts about military sexual assault—the campaign failed to address critical red flags. Key figures like Rebecca Katz of Fight Agency prioritized crafting a 'rugged everyman' image over rigorous background checks, leading to public scandals and eventual resignation of key staff. The piece highlights systemic flaws in the Democratic Party's strategy of recruiting untested outsiders, emphasizing how superficial optics overshadowed substantive vetting. Critics argue that Platner’s team, including de facto campaign manager Moraff, was never fully aligned with the campaign’s goals, further exacerbating the collapse. The scandal underscores broader concerns about the risks of relying on consultants to build candidates without thorou

item.title: Weekly Political Cartoons Highlight Key Issues and Satire
item.description: This article showcases a collection of political cartoons created by cartoonists across the United States, capturing the essence of recent political events through humor, critique, and satire. The cartoons reflect a wide range of perspectives, from bipartisan commentary to sharp critiques of political figures and policies. Each illustration serves as a commentary on current events, blending entertainment with social and political critique. The compilation highlights how visual media can distill complex political narratives into digestible, thought-provoking formats. These works often provoke strong reactions, as they challenge readers to engage with political realities in a more visceral way. The article emphasizes the role of political cartoons as both a mirror and a weapon, reflecting societal issues while also pushing boundaries in public discourse. By aggregating the best of the week’s cartoons, the piece underscores the enduring relevance of satire in political communication and i

item.title: CIA Director John Ratcliffe Faces Scrutiny Over Memo Contradicting Trump's Claims on China Election Interference
item.description: In a heated exchange, MS NOW's David Rohde accused CIA Director John Ratcliffe of failing to explain his memo that contradicts President Donald Trump's claims about China's interference in the 2020 U.S. elections. During a segment on 'Morning Joe,' Rohde highlighted Ratcliffe's presence in a room where Trump accused China of tampering with the election, despite the memo Ratcliffe authored stating otherwise. The memo, dated January 2021, argued that China's efforts to influence the election through propaganda and bots were more significant than the assessment by Representative Jim Himes. Rohde questioned whether Ratcliffe lied, allowed a cover-up, or was part of a 'deep state' plot. The article underscores the growing tensions between Trump's assertions and the CIA's findings, raising concerns about accountability and transparency within the administration. Ratcliffe's dissenting memo, now a public document, has sparked debates about the credibility of his statements and the potential f

item.title: Stock market declines as tech sector struggles with earnings and AI delays
item.description: The U.S. stock market experienced a decline on Thursday, with the S&P 500 losing 0.51% and the Nasdaq Composite dropping 1.47% amid a sell-off in technology stocks. The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.20%, reflecting broader market weakness. Key factors included a delay in Alphabet's Gemini 3.5 Pro AI model, which contributed to a 4% drop in its shares, and a broader sell-off in semiconductor stocks. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) raised its capital expenditure forecast to $60-$64 billion, but its shares fell over 4% as investors reacted to the news. Other tech giants like Micron Technology, Advanced Micro Devices, and Broadcom also saw significant declines. Despite strong earnings reports from 87% of S&P 500 companies, the tech sector's underperformance overshadowed positive results. The Federal Reserve's Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan called for modestly higher interest rates to address persistent inflation concerns. Meanwhile, retail sales and jobless claims

item.title: Actor Sam Neill Dies from Pneumonia at 73
item.description: Actor Sam Neill, renowned for his roles in 'Jurassic Park' and 'The Piano,' passed away at the age of 73 due to pneumonia. His agent confirmed the cause of death and announced that a private family memorial will be held at Neill's New Zealand farm. Neill, who had a long and distinguished career in film and television, was born in 1949 and became a household name through his iconic performances. His death marks the end of an era for fans of his work, which spanned over five decades. The actor's family had previously shared the news of his passing, highlighting his legacy in the entertainment industry. Neill's contributions to cinema, including his role as Ian Malcolm in 'Jurassic Park,' have left a lasting impact on audiences worldwide. The memorial will honor his life and career, celebrating his achievements and the joy he brought to millions of fans. His passing is a significant loss to the film community, and his work continues to be celebrated for its depth and artistry.

item.title: Roblox Introduces AI-Powered Game Creation Tool for Mobile Users
item.description: Roblox has announced a new feature called 'Build' that allows users to create games via text prompts on their mobile devices. This tool leverages both open-source and proprietary AI models to generate basic game frameworks, handling elements like gameplay mechanics, environments, characters, and visuals. While the feature aims to lower barriers for non-programmers, it has sparked concerns among developers about potential influx of low-quality content and increased competition from AI-generated games. Roblox plans to address these issues by ranking AI-created games based on player retention metrics, ensuring only engaging titles receive prominent visibility. The Build tool will enter public alpha testing in New Zealand for users aged 9+, with global publishing options for those 16+. Additional AI developments include agents for playtesting and analytics, as well as a scene-generation model capable of creating editable 3D scenes from text prompts. These innovations underscore Roblox's on

item.title: Google Expands AI Video Creation Tools with Custom Avatars and Enhanced Editing Features
item.description: Google has announced significant updates to its Google Vids platform, transforming it into a comprehensive AI video creation tool. The update allows users to create personalized digital avatars that mimic their appearance and voice using a selfie and voice recording. Additionally, the integration of Gemini Omni, Google's multi-modal AI model, enables users to generate videos by combining written prompts with uploaded reference images. Omni can also perform tasks like background replacement, lighting adjustments, and adding effects, with support for step-by-step edits to refine videos without starting over. These enhancements position Google Vids beyond its original role as a workplace presentation tool, expanding its utility for business applications such as company updates and training videos. However, the platform now competes with other AI video tools like HeyGen and Synthesia, offering personalized avatars and conversational edits. Access to personal avatars is restricted to users

item.title: Rep. Massie advances transparency bill to address Epstein file disclosures
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) and Rep. Ro Khanna (D-CA) are promoting an amendment to their previous Epstein Files Transparency Act (EFTA) to further investigate the Trump administration's handling of Jeffrey Epstein-related documents. The new legislation aims to compel the U.S. attorney general to cooperate with state attorneys general seeking to prosecute Epstein co-conspirators under state law, addressing concerns that the initial bill did not fully expose all relevant files. Critics argue the Trump administration withheld millions of documents and redacted information beyond legal limits. Massie, who lost his GOP primary race to a Trump-backed candidate in May, emphasized the bill's goal of removing executive privileges to withhold records and allowing judicial oversight of compliance with legislative mandates. The amendment follows the release of 3 million documents under the original EFTA but faces challenges from federal agencies resisting full disclosure. The legislation highlights

item.title: Innovative Camera Technology Enables 3D Tracking of Invisible Particles in Scientific Research
item.description: A groundbreaking advancement in imaging technology has been developed by scientists at ETH Zurich, introducing a novel camera system capable of tracking invisible particles in three dimensions. This innovation, detailed in a study published in *Nature Communications*, utilizes a plenoptic-camera system to achieve high-resolution 3D particle tracking without segmenting scintillators. The technology leverages advanced optical design and machine learning algorithms to reconstruct faint light signals, enabling precise tracking of subatomic particles and other invisible entities. The system's potential applications extend beyond particle physics, including medical imaging such as positron emission tomography (PET), where it could enhance diagnostic accuracy by improving spatial resolution. Researchers highlight that this development could revolutionize fields requiring high-precision imaging, from astrophysics to materials science. The study emphasizes the system's ability to achieve sub-mi

item.title: U.S. Tightens Visa Rules for International Students and Journalists
item.description: The Trump administration has implemented new visa regulations that limit the duration of F visas for international students, J visas for cultural exchange visitors, and I visas for journalists. Under the updated rules, student and exchange visas will be capped at four years, while journalist visas will be limited to 240 days (or 90 days for Chinese nationals). The changes, effective 60 days after publication in the federal register, aim to address concerns about the growing number of non-immigrant visa holders. Critics argue the policy creates barriers for international students, who now have only 30 days to secure employment sponsorship after graduation. China condemned the move as discriminatory, citing a 2021 agreement on media cooperation. The Department of Homeland Security cited a 11% increase in student visa admissions in 2024 as justification, claiming the policy will improve oversight of non-immigrants. However, opponents warn the restrictions could push qualified individuals

item.title: Big Bear Teen's Tragic Fall Sparks Plans for Bakery in Her Name
item.description: An 18-year-old woman, Bella Fanuele, died after falling from a bridge support structure in Big Bear, California, on Tuesday night. Identified as a recent high school graduate and aspiring baker, Fanuele was found by rescue workers after deputies received a call about a search and rescue operation. Authorities confirmed there were no suspicious circumstances surrounding her death. Friends and family gathered at the scene to leave flowers at a makeshift memorial, highlighting the area's significance as a local hangout for teens. The victim's family expressed their desire to open a bakery in her name to honor her memory and dreams. The incident has sparked discussions about safety at the site, which has been a popular gathering spot for youth. While the exact cause of the fall remains under investigation, the community is coming together to support the family's initiative to commemorate Fanuele's life through a bakery. The tragedy underscores the importance of addressing potential hazards

item.title: Fruit Fly Sperm Mechanics: How They Avoid Tangling Through Opposite Movement
item.description: A study published in Nature Physics reveals that fruit fly sperm, which are nearly 40 times longer than human sperm, avoid tangling by moving in opposite directions within their storage sacs. Researchers observed that these sperm cells form organized layers, with neighboring cells traveling in opposite directions, creating a coordinated 'traffic' system. This movement prevents the sperm from becoming entangled, allowing them to remain functional despite their size. The study, conducted on Drosophila melanogaster, used advanced imaging techniques to track sperm movement and found that their long tails generate waves that push against each other, enabling sliding motion. The findings shift focus from evolutionary competition to mechanical efficiency, suggesting that this behavior may also maintain sperm organization during the female reproductive tract. The research highlights the complexity of sperm dynamics and their role in reproductive biology.

item.title: Sen. Warner Criticizes Media for Airplay of Trump's Election Fraud Claims, AI's Role in Political Propaganda
item.description: U.S. Senator Mark Warner (D-VA) publicly criticized CBS News for broadcasting Donald Trump's election fraud speech, calling it a 'rehash of falsehoods' and demanding media outlets push back against misinformation. Warner, who was on-air with CBS host Tony Dokoupil, argued that networks have a responsibility to contextualize and fact-check political statements, especially from a president whose claims are 'fact-challenged.' The incident highlights growing tensions between political figures and media outlets over the handling of sensitive topics. Meanwhile, the article also explores the rise of 'slopaganda'—AI-generated political propaganda embraced by conservatives. Analysts note that right-wing groups are leveraging AI to create fake imagery, such as deepfakes of politicians or manipulated media, to amplify inflammatory rhetoric. The Trump administration's ties to Big Tech and AI investors have enabled this trend, with examples including AI-generated images of Trump as Superman or the

item.title: J.D. Vance's Unusual Travel Demands Spark Frustration Among Secret Service Agents
item.description: The article details how J.D. Vance's last-minute, high-cost travel demands have frustrated Secret Service agents assigned to protect him and his family. These include using government helicopters for impromptu trips like a golf lesson at Joint Base Andrews and house-hunting in Middleburg, Virginia. Agents reportedly created a dark joke about the situation, referencing 'Bobcat OTR Survivors Club,' where 'OTR' stands for off-the-record movements that disrupt their plans. Vance's office defended the travel as a 'unique challenge' of protecting young children, but agents claim this level of unpredictability is unprecedented. The article links Vance's behavior to past unprofessional incidents, such as his awkward jokes during meetings with political figures and criticism from Republicans like John Oliver and Andy Beshear. Secret Service supervisors note that career agents rarely complain about their charges, making the current situation particularly unusual.

item.title: Founders Fund Appoints Former OpenAI Executive Ryan Beiermeister as Partner
item.description: Ryan Beiermeister has joined Founders Fund as a partner, marking a significant move in the venture capital space. Her appointment follows her tenure at OpenAI, where she served as VP of Product Policy. Beiermeister's departure from OpenAI was abrupt, reportedly due to her objection to a planned feature called 'adult mode' for ChatGPT. The feature, which aimed to allow adult users for explicit content, faced controversy and was ultimately scrapped. While her firing was linked to a sexual discrimination accusation, Beiermeister denied the allegations, calling them 'absolutely false.' Beyond her professional career, Beiermeister gained attention for her strategic gameplay in the 'Mafia' game, a popular activity at Founders Fund retreats. Though her skills in the game were noted, the firm clarified that it wasn't part of her interview process. Instead, her long-standing relationship with Trae Stephens, a Founders Fund partner, played a role in her hiring. Beiermeister emphasizes her intere

item.title: A Friend's Uncomfortable Truth: Declining a Political Endorsement
item.description: The article addresses the dilemma of declining to endorse a friend for a political position despite their personal bond. A reader seeks guidance on how to tactfully refuse to support a candidate they believe is temperamentally unsuitable for office. The letter highlights the tension between loyalty and honesty, emphasizing the importance of integrity in public service. Miss Manners advises navigating this situation with care, suggesting that while direct criticism may strain relationships, it is necessary to uphold ethical standards. The piece underscores the broader implications of political endorsements, noting how they shape electoral outcomes and public perception. It also touches on the challenges of balancing personal friendships with civic responsibility, especially during election years when such decisions carry significant weight. The discussion reflects on the complexities of modern politics, where individual relationships often intersect with public accountability. Ultimatel

item.title: Vicky Kaushal and Katrina Kaif Celebrate Katrina's 43rd Birthday with Family and Fans
item.description: Vicky Kaushal shared an adorable photo of his wife Katrina Kaif's 43rd birthday celebration on Instagram, captioning it 'Jaan Ka Janamdin' (My love's birthday). The couple, married in December 2021, opted for an intimate family gathering, with fans expressing admiration in the comments. The post features the couple embracing amidst white-and-gold balloons and a 'Happy Birthday' banner, with Vicky holding a cake. Their son, Vihaan, was born in November 2025. The article highlights their preference for privacy while sharing cherished moments, noting fan reactions and their Bollywood fame. It also mentions their wedding at Six Senses Fort Barwara and their efforts to balance personal life with public attention. The piece underscores their affectionate dynamic and the couple's impact on fans, emphasizing their genuine connection despite media scrutiny.

item.title: Opinion Piece Argues the UK's Push for Small Modular Reactors Is Economically and Politically Unsustainable
item.description: This opinion article argues that the United Kingdom has become the leading promoter of small modular reactors (SMRs) despite what the author describes as repeated evidence that the technology is not economically competitive. According to the article, the recent surge in positive media coverage surrounding SMRs is intended to create public confidence in a broader revival of the nuclear industry rather than being driven by proven commercial success. The author points to extensive coverage by British and international media outlets and highlights initiatives such as the High Value Manufacturing Catapult consultation on future nuclear investment as examples of growing institutional support. The article contrasts private-sector proposals for fleets of SMRs with government-backed initiatives, suggesting that advocates are presenting the UK as the global leader in a new nuclear renaissance. It also reviews previous SMR efforts in Russia, China, and the United States, arguing that those projec

item.title: Belfast Hit-and-Run Incident Claims 24-Year-Old Woman's Life, Driver Arrested
item.description: A 24-year-old woman from County Tipperary, Raven Adams, died after being struck by a car in a hit-and-run incident in north Belfast on July 16, 2026. The collision occurred at approximately 2:40 a.m. in the York Street area, leaving the 22-year-old driver of the vehicle fleeing the scene. Emergency services arrived promptly, but Adams was pronounced dead at the scene. The driver was later apprehended in the Cavehill area. A 22-year-old male pedestrian was also seriously injured and hospitalized. Police have appealed for witnesses to come forward, emphasizing the severity of the incident. DUP MLA Philip Brett expressed deep sorrow, noting the community's shock and offering condolences to the victims' families. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about road safety and criminal behavior in the region. Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the driver's decision to flee, which could impact the legal proceedings. This tragic event underscores the need for stricter

item.title: Nintendo Music Adds Donkey Kong Bananza Soundtrack with Over 229 Tracks
item.description: Nintendo Music has recently expanded its library with the full soundtrack of Donkey Kong Bananza, featuring 229 tracks spanning over 8 hours and 36 minutes. This addition to the Switch Online app includes all musical transformations from the game, with Nintendo advising players to activate the 'spoiler block' if they haven't completed the game. The soundtrack is part of a series of updates to the service, which also includes soundtracks for titles like Kirby Air Riders and Mario Kart World. The release highlights Nintendo's commitment to preserving classic game music, with the Donkey Kong series having multiple soundtracks available on the platform. The article notes that while some players appreciate the depth of the soundtrack, others find the game's length and gameplay repetitive. The update requires an active Switch Online subscription to access, emphasizing Nintendo's strategy to monetize its music library through subscription services. This development reflects broader trends in

item.title: George Lucas discusses AI's impact on filmmaking through historical analogy
item.description: In an interview for Rabbit's Foot, Andy Hazel discusses George Lucas's views on generative AI in filmmaking. Lucas, creator of Star Wars and founder of the Lucas Museum of Narrative Art, draws a parallel between the transition from horse-drawn carriages to automobiles and the current shift toward AI technology. He argues that while some may resist technological progress, it is inevitable and represents the future of filmmaking. Lucas acknowledges the challenges of adopting new technologies but emphasizes that innovation is unavoidable. The interview touches on broader implications of AI in creative industries, suggesting that tools like AI will streamline production processes. However, the conversation also raises questions about the balance between technological advancement and artistic integrity. The article highlights Lucas's perspective on how AI could revolutionize storytelling while acknowledging the potential for both opportunity and disruption in the film industry.

item.title: Émile Vernon: Romantic French Artist of the Belle Époque
item.description: Émile Vernon (1872–1919) was a French artist renowned for his delicate and romantic style during the Belle Époque period. His works, primarily created in watercolor and gouache, captured the elegance and poetic mood of early 20th-century French art through idealized portraits of graceful young women. These paintings, often set in soft, dreamy environments adorned with flowers and gentle lighting, reflect a timeless elegance and sentimental charm. Vernon’s mastery of romantic art is exemplified by pieces like 'A Summer Rose,' 'A Sweet Glance,' and 'The Pink Rose,' which blend graceful figures with dreamy atmospheres. His art continues to enchant viewers, offering a window into the refined aesthetic of the Belle Époque. The article also highlights his focus on themes like femininity, love, and nature, showcasing how his work remains relevant in appreciating the cultural and artistic movements of his time.

item.title: Wildlife Behavior Varies Based on Human Threat Type
item.description: A study conducted by researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) reveals that animals exhibit distinct behavioral responses to different types of human threats. The research highlights how species react differently to lethal versus non-lethal interactions with humans, challenging the common perception of humans as an overarching threat to wildlife. For instance, while many animals flee from human presence, others display curiosity or indifference. The study also found that certain environments, such as roads and settlements, can act as refuges for some species, altering traditional predator-prey dynamics. These findings have significant implications for conservation strategies and wildlife management practices. By understanding how animals perceive and respond to human activity, researchers can develop more effective measures to mitigate conflicts between humans and wildlife. The study emphasizes the importance of context in shaping animal behavior, suggesting that simplistic

item.title: Exploring Japanese Retro-Futurism of the 1960s: Nostalgia Meets Visionary Design
item.description: The article delves into the unique allure of Japanese retro-futurism from the 1960s, highlighting how it merges nostalgia with visionary design elements distinct from Western counterparts. Unlike Western styles that emphasize sleek, chrome-heavy aesthetics, Japanese retro-futurism incorporates neon cityscapes, chunky analog technology, and dramatic robotics. The piece showcases images reflecting space futurism, bubble economy optimism, and fantastical rescue craft, all rooted in the 1960s era. These visuals capture a sense of intensity that amplifies their impact, offering a glimpse into a speculative future imagined during that time. The article also references its inclusion in 'Retrofuturism: The Future We Were Promised,' a visual guide to past visions of tomorrow. By blending historical context with creative design, the piece underscores how retro-futurism serves as both a reflection of cultural aspirations and a bridge between past and future innovation.

item.title: Ongoing US-Iran Conflict Escalates with Continued Airstrikes and Regional Attacks
item.description: The US and Iran continue their escalating conflict, with the US conducting its sixth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iran and Iran retaliating by targeting Gulf states. US Central Command confirmed the latest wave of strikes, which focused on southern Iran but also reached deep into the country. Iran accused the US of damaging critical infrastructure, including bridges near Bandar Abbas, a key location on the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan reported fresh attacks, with Qatar sustaining casualties from shrapnel. The Iranian military claimed to have targeted US forces in Syria and Oman, though these claims remain unverified. President Trump addressed the nation to discuss the conflict but did not outline a clear path forward. Iran's Energy Ministry urged citizens to limit air conditioning use to stabilize electricity supply after attacks disrupted power lines. Oil prices remained stable as the White House dismissed concerns about market disruptions

item.title: Why More Kids Can't Access Summer Learning Programs in Maryland
item.description: The article discusses the importance of summer learning programs in Maryland, emphasizing that these programs should serve as a bridge to better opportunities rather than just a break from school. Published on July 17, 2026, the piece highlights the need for equitable access to educational resources during the summer months. It critiques the current system's failure to provide all children with the chance to benefit from these programs, which can alleviate academic stress and offer enriching experiences. The author argues that summer learning should be a priority for policymakers and educators to ensure that all students, regardless of socioeconomic background, have access to quality educational opportunities. The article also mentions the role of Maryland Matters, a nonprofit media coalition, in covering such issues. While the piece acknowledges the potential of summer as a time for growth and exploration, it calls for systemic changes to address disparities in access to these program

item.title: SpaceX aborts Starship V3 launch due to engine failure
item.description: SpaceX aborted its second attempt to launch the upgraded Starship V3 rocket system on July 16, 2026, just moments after ignition at its South Texas facility. CEO Elon Musk confirmed on X that four of the new Raptor engines failed to start, triggering an automatic abort. The company plans to replace two engines and delay further attempts until next week. This incident follows a mixed May test flight where the Super Heavy booster stage failed during a simulated landing, prompting an FAA review. The aborted launch comes amid a decline in SpaceX’s stock price, which fell below its IPO price of $135 after the failure. The mission aimed to deploy third-generation Starlink satellites, though these are designed to burn up in the atmosphere. The failure marks another setback for SpaceX’s ambitious plans to establish orbital data centers, with Starlink being its sole profitable business segment. The incident highlights ongoing challenges in perfecting the Starship system, which remains critical

item.title: Dave Kendall, MTV's 120 Minutes Creator, Passes Away, Leaving Legacy in Music Journalism
item.description: Dave Kendall, the creator and host of MTV's iconic music video show 120 Minutes, has passed away. His former colleague Matt Pinfield announced the news on social media, noting that Kendall's death marks the end of an era for alternative music. Kendall, who was 68 according to his Wikipedia page, played a pivotal role in launching the careers of numerous artists during the 1990s. His 1991 debut of Nirvana's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' video was a watershed moment for grunge and alternative rock, propelling the genre into mainstream popularity. Despite his passing, Kendall's legacy endures through the cultural impact of 120 Minutes, which aired for over a decade and introduced fans to groundbreaking music. Pinfield, who later took over as host of the show, praised Kendall's dedication to music and his ability to connect with fans authentically. Beyond MTV, Kendall continued his career in music journalism through SiriusXM and the Bangkok Post, leaving a lasting influence on the industry. Hi

item.title: Coca-Cola Halts Fairlife Dairy Production After Ransomware Attack
item.description: Coca-Cola, a global beverage giant, has temporarily suspended production at its Fairlife dairy subsidiary following a ransomware attack that disrupted its operations. The company disclosed the incident to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, stating that its Fairlife production systems are affected, though operations in Canada remain unaffected. Fairlife, a major brand under Coca-Cola with an estimated $4 billion in sales by 2024, faces potential long-term impacts from the cyberattack. This incident follows similar ransomware attacks on food and beverage companies, such as Arizona Beverages in 2019 and UNFI last year, which caused weeks of production delays and grocery shortages. Coca-Cola has not provided a timeline for system restoration, raising concerns about supply chain disruptions and consumer access to dairy products. The attack underscores the growing threat of cybercrime to critical industries, highlighting the need for robust cybersecurity measures. The company’s res

item.title: Ben Shapiro Criticizes JD Vance for Embracing Democratic Policies
item.description: Conservative media personality Ben Shapiro criticized Vice President JD Vance for adopting policies reminiscent of Democrats during his appearance on Joe Rogan's podcast. Shapiro accused Vance of echoing Bernie Sanders' economic views, Barack Obama's foreign policy, and Ro Khanna's conspiracy theories, arguing that such alignment undermines the GOP's 2028 presidential prospects. Vance, however, defended Trump against Epstein allegations, denounced socialism, and criticized Israel's influence on U.S. foreign policy. Despite Shapiro's concerns about Vance's ideological drift, his own influence in right-wing media has declined significantly since 2023, prompting The Daily Wire to downsize. The article highlights the growing tensions within the conservative movement, with Shapiro warning about rising antisemitism and questioning Vance's ability to lead the MAGA faction. It also underscores the broader debate over ideological purity versus pragmatic governance in the Republican Party.

item.title: Arizona GOP Candidate Faces Primary Challenge Over Sexual Scandals and Trump Endorsement
item.description: Mark Lamb, a Republican candidate for Arizona's House of Representatives, is facing a contentious primary challenge due to allegations of sexual misconduct. His opponent, Daniel Keenan, has spent millions to highlight these scandals, while Lamb's campaign has not invested in advertising. The race has drawn attention to the influence of former President Donald Trump's endorsement, which has bolstered Lamb's position despite the controversy. The allegations, which involve inappropriate conduct with multiple individuals, have sparked debates within the GOP about the role of character in political candidacy. Lamb's supporters argue that his strong performance in Pinal County during the 2024 Senate race demonstrates his viability, while critics question his judgment. The race also reflects broader tensions within the party, as some lawmakers have distanced themselves from Lamb, citing concerns about his behavior. The primary, moved to late July, faces low turnout due to summer travel patter

item.title: Political Debate Over Epstein Files and Trump's Legal Involvement
item.description: Two political commentators, Brian Tyler Cohen and Jason Rantz, engaged in a heated argument during a late-night panel discussion regarding the Epstein files and their implications for former President Donald Trump. Cohen argued that Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche was elevated due to his role in protecting Trump from potential legal consequences related to the Epstein files. Rantz countered by asserting that Trump is not criminally implicated in the case, emphasizing that no evidence exists to suggest he committed a crime. The debate escalated when Cohen questioned whether Rantz had seen all 3 million files, prompting Rantz to acknowledge partial access while reiterating his stance on the lack of criminal charges under the Biden administration. Cohen pushed for transparency, citing the conviction of Epstein accomplice Ghislaine Maxwell under Biden's leadership, while Rantz accused Cohen of selectively advocating for action only recently. The exchange highlighted deepening partisan

item.title: Defense Secretary's Testosterone Plan Faces Scientific Criticism
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has proposed annual testosterone screenings for troops aged 30 and older, with voluntary hormone therapy for those deemed deficient. The plan aims to restore a 'warrior ethos' by optimizing natural capabilities, but experts like Adrian Dobs from Johns Hopkins University argue it oversimplifies complex endocrinology. Dobs highlights issues such as circadian rhythm variations in testosterone levels, the impact of stress on hormone production, and the risks of testosterone replacement therapy, including testicular atrophy, reduced sperm count, and cardiovascular risks. The proposal has drawn criticism for lacking scientific justification and ignoring underlying health conditions. Hegseth's policies also include purging transgender service members and imposing 'male standard' fitness tests, raising concerns about discriminatory practices. The article underscores the lack of concrete evidence supporting the plan's benefits and questions its alignment with m

item.title: Trump Uses 1990s Computer Game Photo to Support Election Meddling Claims
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's recent speech on election security included a controversial reference to a 1990s computer game, 'Doom,' as part of a supposed evidence dump about election meddling. According to a report by Talking Points Memo, the documents Trump shared during his address featured a 'wild' anecdote about the game, which was linked to a hacked pollbook demonstration at the 2019 Defcon Voting Machine Hacking Village. The memo, declassified by former Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, described a 'Pollbook Hacking Example' involving 'Doom,' though the photo was based on press reporting from the event. The image, originally from a tweet by user l33tLumberjack, was included in a Washington Post article about the hacking conference. Critics, including the National Association of Secretaries of State, called the demonstration 'unrealistic,' highlighting the disconnect between the game's context and the claims of election interference. Trump's use of the photo has s

item.title: Common Mistakes Foreign Developer Tools Make in Entering the Korean Market
